title: Plate Cell – SELEX Protocol
author: Claudia Troncone Clemente
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8sehwbe
materials: SELEX INITIAL ROUND: Exposure and Screening

steps.title: In 1mL of LB liquid medium, resuspend the E
steps.instruction: In 1mL of LB liquid medium, resuspend the E. coli cells. Incubate it at 37 °C overnight with constant shaking at 180 rpm in a Incubator-shaker

steps.title: Take a sterile microtiter plate and...
steps.instruction: Take a sterile microtiter plate and coat it with 100 μl of 2.5 mM PBA prepared in carbonate buffer (pH 9.2). Incubate the plate overnight at 4ºC. This will make the well surface to functionalize with PBA.

steps.title: Add 10 μL of 100 uM...
steps.instruction: Add 10 μL of 100 uM ssDNA library to 190 ul of binding buffer. Incubate it at 95 °C for 10 min. After that, transfer immediately to ice-bath to prevent rehybridization of the single stranded DNA library. If the transfer is poor performed, you can reheat again for another 10 min and repeat. Incubate the tube at 4 °C overnight (in the refrigerator). This will allow the single stranded DNA oligos to take their respective 3D conformations.

steps.title: With the spectrophotometer set at λ...
steps.instruction: With the spectrophotometer set at λ = 600 nm, measure the growth of the inoculum. It should be ≥ 1. If it's lower, it should be left growing until it reaches this value.

steps.title: Take 250 μL of bacteria and...
steps.instruction: Take 250 μL of bacteria and inoculate it in 25 ml LB media. (use 1% O.D. as an inoculum for subculturing)

steps.title: Incubate at 37ºC, 180 rpm for 1
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37ºC, 180 rpm for 1.5h (until the culture reaches an O.D. between 0.5 – 0.6, which will indicate that the culture is in log-phase)

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 3000 X g for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 3000 X g for 10 minutes

steps.title: Remove supernatant and then wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and then wash the cells x3 with PBS (10 mM, pH 7.4). This will remove residual components from the previous step.

steps.title: Measure the O
steps.instruction: Measure the O.D. at =600 nm using a spectrophotometer at λ = 600 nm. The optical density (O.D.) should be ≥ 1. This corresponds approximately to 1 x 10 8 E. coli cells (diluted in PBS). Further dilutions can be done by adding PBS to tubes.

steps.title: Wash the PBA coated plate x2 with carbonate buffer
steps.instruction: Wash the PBA coated plate x2 with carbonate buffer. Then, resuspend 100 μL of 1 x 10 3 cells in well #1.

steps.title: Incubate at 25 ºC for 1h
steps.instruction: Incubate at 25 ºC for 1h. This will allow the cells to coat the surface of the well

steps.title: Decant the suspension by inversion and...
steps.instruction: Decant the suspension by inversion and wash the well with 200 μL of Binding buffer (x3). The washing will remove the cells that didn't bind to the surface.

steps.title: Incubate 200 μL of naïve library...
steps.instruction: Incubate 200 μL of naïve library (prepared the previous day) at 37ºC for 15 minutes

steps.title: Add 200 μL of library to...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of library to the cell coated well (gently!) and then incubate at 37ºC for 1h.

steps.title: Decant the solution from the plate by inverting
steps.instruction: Decant the solution from the plate by inverting.

steps.title: Wash x3 with 200 ul of...
steps.instruction: Wash x3 with 200 ul of Binding buffer to separate loosely bound sequences

steps.title: Add 200 μL of elution buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of elution buffer to the well and then incubate at room temperature for 5 min.

steps.title: Remove the solution by using micropipette...
steps.instruction: Remove the solution by using micropipette (be careful not to touch the walls) and neutralize inmediately by adding 10 μL of neutralization buffer. This will bring the pH to ~7.

steps.title: Quantify the obtained sequences with the...
steps.instruction: Quantify the obtained sequences with the spectrophotometer and label as Round #1. To avoid bacterial contamination due to detachment, the DNA elutes can be centrifuged at 3,000 x g for 10 min and take the supernatant prior to quantification. This can be stored at -20 °C till further use.
